title: Sweet Smoked Brazilian Sweet Potato Stew
description: A hearty and comforting Brazilian dish featuring sweet potatoes, muscovado sugar, and smoky wok-hei flavors, made with ayurvedic-sattvic-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Heat the coconut oil in a large wok or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the diced sweet potatoes, smoked paprika, and muscovado sugar, and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the sweet potatoes are tender.
Use an immersion blender to puree the stew to the desired consistency.
Season with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.
Garnish with fresh herbs, if desired.
